entry.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
entry.summary:
In the discourse on securing cryptocurrency transactions, particularly in the context of Lightning networks which predominantly use `SIGHASH_NOINPUT`-based mechanisms, a highlighted security strategy involves monitoring onchain transactions rather than mempool events. This approach is advocated due to the inherent risk posed by transaction malleation in unconfirmed transaction pools. Once a transaction secures confirmation on the blockchain, its transaction ID (txid) remains stable unless affected by a blockchain reorganization (reorg), which in itself is a rare event.
To further mitigate risks associated with deep blockchain reorganizations, it is suggested that transactions continue to be rebroadcast with updated prevouts until they achieve deep confirmation. This ensures that even if a reorg were to occur, the integrity and traceability of the transactions remain intact. Additionally, employing strategies such as scorch-the-earth or keep-bumping-the-fee can enhance security. These strategies involve repeatedly broadcasting new versions of a spending transaction using higher fees to ensure its persistence in subsequent blocks, thereby guarding against potential double-spend attacks or similar exploitations.
The discussion encapsulates the necessity for robust monitoring and management strategies in cryptocurrency transactions, especially within systems leveraging `SIGHASH_NOINPUT`, to uphold transaction integrity and prevent potential attack vectors. The focus is squarely on adapting to the nuances of blockchain dynamics to safeguard transactions against the vulnerabilities inherent in digital currency systems.
